binaries.title: ksrext.sys - memory-partition-list ownership and reference counting refactored into KsrPersistMemoryPartition

changed_functions.name: KsrPersistMemoryPartition
key_changes: Added ObReferenceObjectByHandle(Handle, 2, PsPartitionType, 0, &Object, nullptr) after ObOpenObjectByPointerWithTag; all subsequent queries (ObQueryNameString, ObGetObjectSecurity, KsrpQueryMemoryRuns, KsrpQueryIoSpaceRuns) now use the referenced Object instead of the caller pointer a2

Object and handle fields of the descriptor passed to the external routine KsrpPersistMemoryPartitionRoutinePointer are now cleared (unpatched set descriptor[3]=a2 and descriptor[9]=Handle; patched sets both to 0)
List insertion is now performed inline by the new helper KsrpAddPartitionToList(Handle, Object, arg1); on success the handle and reference are transferred to the list entry (both set to NULL)
Reference released on failure via ObfDereferenceObjectWithTag(Object, 0x2072734B)
Partition-list lock changed from shared (ExAcquirePushLockSharedEx / KsrpPdDatabaseLock) to exclusive (ExAcquirePushLockExclusiveEx / KsrpPartitionLock) because the driver now writes the list
changed_functions.security_notes: Not a security-relevant fix. In the unpatched build the driver does not store the caller's raw partition pointer in its own persistent list and does not take its own object reference; it hands the raw object pointer and the opened handle to an externally registered loader routine (KsrpPersistMemoryPartitionRoutinePointer) that owns the insertion and lifetime. During the call the PsPartitionType object is kept alive by the caller's reference and by the freshly opened handle, so the in-function uses of a2 are not use-after-free. The patched build takes over persisted-partition-list ownership: it takes its own reference through the handle, uses the referenced pointer for all queries, performs the insertion inline via the new KsrpAddPartitionToList, and releases the reference in error paths. The added ObReferenceObjectByHandle/ObfDereferenceObjectWithTag are the natural consequence of moving insertion and ownership into the driver (matching the ObfDereferenceObject/ZwClose teardown already present in KsrpDestoryPartitionList), not a repair of a dangling pointer present in the unpatched driver. No attacker-reachable use-after-free is demonstrable from these binaries.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED (0x1C0008780):
  0x1C00088D7: mov rcx, r12                 ; Object = a2
  0x1C00088DA: call ObOpenObjectByPointerWithTag
  0x1C0008907: mov rcx, r12 ; -> ObQueryNameString(a2,...)
  0x1C000897D: mov rcx, r12 ; -> ObGetObjectSecurity(a2,...)
  0x1C000899B: mov rcx, r12 ; -> KsrpQueryMemoryRuns(a2,...)
  0x1C00089B6: mov rcx, r12 ; -> KsrpQueryIoSpaceRuns(a2,...)
  0x1C00089F1: mov [rbp+var_118], r12       ; descriptor object field = a2
  0x1C0008A01: mov [rbp+var_E8], rax        ; descriptor handle field
  0x1C0008A45: call __guard_dispatch_icall_fptr ; external KsrpPersistMemoryPartitionRoutinePointer(descriptor)
  0x1C0008A83: call ZwClose                 ; failure cleanup, handle only; no object deref; no KsrpAddPartitionToList

PATCHED (0x14000A820):
  0x14000A94F: call ObOpenObjectByPointerWithTag
  0x14000A98D: call ObReferenceObjectByHandle ; ADDED (Handle, 2, PsPartitionType, 0, &Object)
  0x14000A999: mov rsi, [rbp+Object]         ; rsi = referenced object, used for all queries
  0x14000AA99: and [rbp+var_118], r12        ; descriptor object field cleared (r12=0)
  0x14000AA9D: and [rbp+var_E8], r12         ; descriptor handle field cleared
  0x14000AAF3: call _guard_dispatch_icall     ; external routine(descriptor)
  0x14000AB0A: call KsrpAddPartitionToList    ; ADDED inline insertion (Handle, rsi, arg1)
  0x14000AB15/AB1B: handle and object set to 0 on success (ownership transferred)
  0x14000AB64: call ObfDereferenceObjectWithTag ; ADDED release on failure

changed_functions.name: KsrRegisterLoaderCallbacks
key_changes: Context/registration structure expanded to accommodate soft-restart persistence callback slots

changed_functions.name: KsrMmAllocatePagesForMdlEx
key_changes: arg6/a8 masked with 0x7FFFFFFF before use (mask present only in the patched build)
changed_functions.security_notes: Minor hardening: the masked value flows into MmAllocatePagesForMdlEx, which validates its own inputs. Not an exploitable condition in the unpatched build.

changed_functions.name: KsrQueryPersistedMemoryPartition
key_changes: Read-path lock changed from exclusive (ExAcquirePushLockExclusiveEx @0x1C0008BF7) to shared (ExAcquirePushLockSharedEx @0x14000AF0F)

changed_functions.name: KsrQueryMetadata
key_changes: Global function-pointer indirection refactored into a wrapper call

changed_functions.name: KsrInitPageDatabase
key_changes: Added soft-restart persistence / state-restoration code path

changed_functions.name: KsrEnumeratePersistedMemoryPartitions
key_changes: Added soft-restart partition enumeration path with active/persisted filtering

security_findings.entry_point: Internal driver routine registered through the KSR loader interface (guard: KsrpPersistMemoryPartitionRoutinePointer). No unprivileged user-mode path to this routine is demonstrated by these binaries.
call_chain: unpatched: external KsrpPersistMemoryPartitionRoutinePointer(descriptor) owns list insertion and object lifetime
patched: ksrext.sys!KsrpAddPartitionToList (inline insertion with driver-held reference)
security_findings.description: Prior classification as a Use-After-Free / missing reference count is not supported by the binaries. The unpatched KsrPersistMemoryPartition opens a handle to the caller-supplied PsPartitionType object, queries its name/security/memory-runs using the caller pointer while the object is kept alive by the caller's reference and the open handle, and hands the raw object pointer and handle to an externally registered loader routine (KsrpPersistMemoryPartitionRoutinePointer) via a stack descriptor (descriptor[3]=a2, descriptor[9]=Handle). The driver itself performs no persistent list insertion and takes no independent reference in the unpatched build; the persisted-partition list is populated/owned elsewhere. The patched build moves that ownership into the driver: it adds ObReferenceObjectByHandle to obtain an independently referenced object, uses it for all queries, clears the object/handle fields in the descriptor passed to the external routine, inserts into KsrpMemoryPartitionList inline via the new KsrpAddPartitionToList, transfers ownership of the reference and handle to the list entry on success, and releases the reference via ObfDereferenceObjectWithTag on failure. This matches the teardown already present in both builds (KsrpDestoryPartitionList dereferences [entry+0x10] and closes [entry+0x18]). No dangling pointer exists in the unpatched driver and no attacker-reachable memory-safety fault is demonstrable.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: No reachable trigger established. Within KsrPersistMemoryPartition the object is referenced by both the caller and an open handle for the duration of the call. The unpatched driver does not store an unreferenced raw pointer in its own list, and no function in these images is shown to dereference a list-stored raw pointer after the object could be freed.
security_findings.primitive: None demonstrable. The unpatched driver does not persist a raw, unreferenced object pointer in its own list; the lifetime/insertion behavior that the patched build brings inline is performed by an external loader routine that is not part of these binaries and cannot be shown to omit a reference.
security_findings.assembly:
; UNPATCHED KsrPersistMemoryPartition @ 0x1C0008780
0x1C0008856: call ExAcquirePushLockSharedEx        ; KsrpPdDatabaseLock (read path)
0x1C0008862: mov rax, cs:KsrpMemoryPartitionList
0x1C0008872: cmp [rax+10h], r12                    ; entry object == a2 ?
0x1C00088D7: mov rcx, r12                           ; Object = a2
0x1C00088DA: call ObOpenObjectByPointerWithTag
0x1C0008907: mov rcx, r12 ; 0x1C000890A: call ObQueryNameString
0x1C000897D: mov rcx, r12 ; 0x1C0008980: call ObGetObjectSecurity
0x1C000899B: mov rcx, r12 ; 0x1C00089A2: call KsrpQueryMemoryRuns
0x1C00089B6: mov rcx, r12 ; 0x1C00089BD: call KsrpQueryIoSpaceRuns
0x1C00089F1: mov [rbp+var_118], r12                ; descriptor object field = a2
0x1C0008A01: mov [rbp+var_E8], rax                 ; descriptor handle field
0x1C0008A45: call __guard_dispatch_icall_fptr       ; KsrpPersistMemoryPartitionRoutinePointer(descriptor)
0x1C0008A83: call ZwClose                          ; failure cleanup, handle only

; PATCHED KsrPersistMemoryPartition @ 0x14000A820
0x14000A8EA: call ExAcquirePushLockExclusiveEx     ; KsrpPartitionLock (write path)
0x14000A94F: call ObOpenObjectByPointerWithTag
0x14000A98D: call ObReferenceObjectByHandle        ; ADDED
0x14000A999: mov rsi, [rbp+Object]                 ; referenced object used for all queries
0x14000AA99: and [rbp+var_118], r12                ; descriptor object field cleared
0x14000AA9D: and [rbp+var_E8], r12                 ; descriptor handle field cleared
0x14000AAF3: call _guard_dispatch_icall             ; external routine(descriptor)
0x14000AB0A: call KsrpAddPartitionToList           ; ADDED inline insertion
0x14000AB64: call ObfDereferenceObjectWithTag      ; ADDED release on failure
0x14000AB7A: call ZwClose

breakpoints.why: Entry to the routine under review. rdx (a2) is the caller-supplied PsPartitionType object pointer; it is referenced by the caller and by the handle opened at 0x1C00088DA for the duration of the call.
Teardown path that dereferences [entry+0x10] (ObfDereferenceObject) and closes [entry+0x18] (ZwClose), confirming list entries own a reference and handle in both builds.
New in the patched build: allocates the 0x30-byte list entry and stores the referenced object at +0x10 and the handle at +0x18 before linking it into KsrpMemoryPartitionList.

watch: unpatched 0x1C00089F1: [rbp+var_118] = a2 (raw object handed to the external routine)
unpatched 0x1C0008A45: KsrpPersistMemoryPartitionRoutinePointer dispatch (external routine owns insertion/lifetime)
key_offsets: 0x1C00088DA: ObOpenObjectByPointerWithTag(a2, ...) - opens handle that keeps the object alive during the call
0x1C0008A45: external KsrpPersistMemoryPartitionRoutinePointer dispatch (unpatched owner of persistent insertion)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Not applicable. No attacker-reachable trigger for a memory-safety fault is demonstrated by these binaries; the object is referenced throughout the call and the driver stores no unreferenced raw pointer in its own list.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: None. No fault path is reachable in the unpatched build from the evidence available.
debugging_aids.notes: The partition object is a PsPartitionType object. KsrpMemoryPartitionList entries hold the object at +0x10 and the handle at +0x18. The unpatched base is 0x1C0000000; the patched base is 0x140000000 (addresses are not directly comparable between builds). The external KsrpPersistMemoryPartitionRoutinePointer routine is registered from a separate loader module and is not present in these binaries, so its reference handling cannot be observed here.

summary: No security-relevant change. The most substantive difference, in KsrPersistMemoryPartition, moves ownership of the persisted memory-partition list from an externally registered loader routine (KsrpPersistMemoryPartitionRoutinePointer) into ksrext.sys itself. The unpatched driver opens a handle to the caller-supplied PsPartitionType object, queries it while the object is kept alive by the caller's reference and the open handle, and hands the raw object pointer and handle to the external routine, which owns the persistent insertion and lifetime; the driver takes no independent reference and stores nothing in its own list. The patched driver adds ObReferenceObjectByHandle to hold its own reference, uses the referenced pointer for all queries, clears the object/handle fields passed to the external routine, performs the list insertion inline via the new KsrpAddPartitionToList, transfers ownership on success, and releases the reference via ObfDereferenceObjectWithTag on failure. This matches the teardown already present in both builds (KsrpDestoryPartitionList). No dangling pointer exists in the unpatched driver and no attacker-reachable use-after-free or other primitive is demonstrable. The remaining changed functions are renames/relocations, pool-API modernization, lock-scope adjustments, tracing changes, and soft-restart feature paths.
